mnemonics: My Apple Can Keep Repeating (Markov: A state's future depends only on Current state, not the full history)
keyPoints: A Markov Chain is memoryless: the next state depends only on the current state (Markov Property)
Defined by a transition probability matrix P, where P_ij = probability of moving from state i to state j
Steady-state distribution represents the long-term probabilities of being in each state
commonMistakes: Assuming Markov Chains have memory of more than one previous state
Confusing transition probabilities (e.g., P(A|B) vs P(B|A))
Forgetting that rows of the transition matrix must sum to 1
